query: What fire safety and stroke recognition training does Sunrise Medical Center provide to staff, including training videos, audio announcements, written guides, and diagrams?

vector_search.text:
Sunrise Medical Center Employee Handbook
Sunrise Medical Center
Document Title: Sunrise Medical Center Employee Handbook
Document ID: SMC-HR-001
Department: Human Resources
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Last Revised: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Chief Human Resources Officer, Yolanda Pierce
Distribution: All Sunrise Medical Center Staff
__________________________________________________________________________________________
1. Welcome to Sunrise Medical Center
Welcome to Sunrise Medical Center. For over four decades, our hospital has served the community with a commitment to compassionate, high-quality care. This handbook describes the policies, benefits, and expectations that apply to every employee, from bedside clinical staff to administrative and support teams

Welcome to Sunrise Medical Center. My name is Yolanda Pierce, Chief Human Resources Officer, and on behalf of our entire leadership team, I want to personally welcome you to our hospital family. For over four decades, Sunrise Medical Center has served as community with compassion, clinical excellence, and an unwavering commitment to patient safety. Over the next two days, you will complete orientation covering our mission and values. HIPA is privacy training, patient safety goals, infection prevention, and fire and emergency preparedness. Please keep your identification bash visible at all times, and do not hesitate to ask questions. We are glad you are here, and we look forward to supporting your success in Sunrise Medical Center.

==============================================================================
SUNRISE MEDICAL CENTER

Sepsis Recognition and Early Treatment Protocol
Sunrise Medical Center
Document Title: Sepsis Recognition and Early Treatment Protocol
Document ID: SMC-ED-027
Department: Emergency Department / Infectious Disease
Effective Date: September 15, 2022
Last Revised: April 4, 2025
Policy Owner: Sepsis Program Coordinator, Denise Falk, RN
Distribution: All Sunrise Medical Center Staff
1. Purpose
Sepsis is a leading cause of preventable in-hospital mortality. This protocol establishes the screening tools,
escalation pathway, and time-bound treatment bundle used at Sunrise Medical Center to identify sepsis
early and initiate treatment before irreversible organ damage occurs.
2

Suspected Acute Stroke Protocol — Emergency
 Department
Sunrise Medical Center
Document Title: Suspected Acute Stroke Protocol — Emergency Department
Document ID: SMC-ED-014
Department: Emergency Department / Neurology
Effective Date: March 3, 2023
Last Revised: February 10, 2026
Policy Owner: Director of Emergency Medicine, Dr. Anita Reyes
Distribution: All Sunrise Medical Center Staff
1. Purpose
This protocol establishes the standardized process for the identification, triage, imaging, and treatment of
patients presenting to the Sunrise Medical Center Emergency Department (ED) with signs and symptoms of
an acute stroke

Workforce Training
All new employees, contractors, and volunteers complete HIPAA privacy and security training before
receiving system access, with annual refresher training mandatory for continued access. Training
completion is tracked in the Learning Management System and audited quarterly by Human Resources and
Compliance.
8. Sanctions for Non-Compliance
Violation Category
Example
Typical Sanction
Unintentional/first occurrence
Accessing a coworker's record out of curiosity, self-reported
Formal counseling, retraining
Repeated or negligent
Leaving PHI visible on unattended workstation repeatedly
Written warning, suspension
Willful/malicious
Selling or disclosing PHI for personal gain
Termination, referral to law enforcement
9

==============================================================================
SUNRISE MEDICAL CENTER
HOSPITAL-WIDE EMERGENCY AND DEPARTMENT CONTACT LIST
==============================================================================

Document Title: Hospital-Wide Emergency and Department Contact List

Distribution: All Sunrise Medical Center Staff

suspected stroke recognition training. Remember fast. F. Face-rupping. Ask the patient to smile and check if one side drifts. Make our weakness. Ask the patient to raise both arms and check for drift. F. Speech difficulty. Ask the patient to repeat a phrase and listen for swearing. T. Time to call a code stroke. If any sign is present, note the time and activate a code stroke immediately. It's immediate right.

Patients with confirmed large-vessel occlusion on CT Angiography who present within 24 hours of last
known well time (per extended-window imaging criteria) should be evaluated for transfer to the affiliated
Neurointerventional Center at Riverbend Regional, as Sunrise Medical Center does not currently perform
mechanical thrombectomy on site.
The transfer coordinator must be notified simultaneously with the neurology consult to avoid delay; transport
arrangements should never wait for tPA infusion to complete when thrombectomy is indicated.
8. Documentation Requirements
All Code Stroke events must be documented on the Stroke Core Measures worksheet, including every
timestamp from EMS dispatch through disposition
